framework_constants.core_insight: The parent does NOT have a potty training problem. They have a BODY SIGNAL TRANSLATION PROBLEM.

conversion_levers.L1: Sensory Gap — You haven't failed. You solved the wrong problem.
conversion_levers.L2: Pull-Ups Harm — Pull-ups keep kids completely dry. That's the problem.
conversion_levers.L3: 60-Day Guarantee — Try it for 60 days. By your judgment.
conversion_levers.L4: Lena Peer Trust — Both my sons went through this. Here's what helped.
conversion_levers.L5: 49% SPARK Stat — Almost 1 in 2 autistic kids this age aren't trained.

cross_level_rules_R1_R6: R1: Symptom over label — never name the level, name the experience
R2: 3-5 recognition anchors per mail — so all levels see themselves
R3: Age ranges over fixed ages — 'kids 3 to 10+' not '5-year-olds'
R4: Verbal/non-verbal inclusivity — include both modes in examples
R5: Timeline flexibility — 'some kids 2 weeks, others 8. Both normal.'
R6: Low-bar success metrics — mini-wins, not 'fully trained'
framework_constants.lena_identity: Lena S. — Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons (one 6, one trained at almost 8)

must_include: BCBA respect language in ALL emails

welcome.name: Welcome Flow
welcome.structure_note: S02 §4.1 provides 3 separate per-email tables (L1/L2/L3, 8 emails each = 24) plus a GF summary paragraph covering all 8 GF emails without per-email detail. GF details inferred from §1 framework + R1-R6 + summary paragraph.
emotional_arc: E2: 'Holy shit, that explains it' (mechanism revelation — tipping point)
E3: Hopeful-but-cautious (60-day guarantee as hope shield)
E7: Decisive (last call / permission not to buy)

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Welcome + WELCOME10 code. Mirror: 'He talks about the potty but doesn't produce.' Lena intro: mom of two sons.
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Keep it light. She still has hope. No heavy mechanism yet.

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Mechanism explain: 'He gets it but doesn't get it — that's a signal-timing gap.' Introduce interoception gently. Name 2-3 failed methods (Oh Crap, sticker charts).
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: THE TIPPING POINT EMAIL ('holy shit' moment). L1-specific: avoid BCBA language, focus on timing.

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Decision time: 60-day guarantee + permission not to buy. 'You don't have to hope — just try.' Pre-K anchor allowed.
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Soft Pre-K urgency OK. '60 days before Pre-K = enough time.'

l1_emails.copy_strategy: 'You didn't fail. You solved the wrong problem.' Deep failure acknowledgment. Validate that all methods assumed a signal.
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: She may still be hesitating. Absolution is the key.

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Pre-K countdown (seasonal). 'Most L1 families see first signals in 4-8 weeks.' Concrete timeline.
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Urgency OK for L1 only. Give her a plan: 8-week window.

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Social proof: L1-specific testimonials. 'Almost trained' loop closure stories. Three-Family Pattern if GF text block.
l1_emails.key_emotional_tactic: She needs to see other L1 moms succeeded.

l1_emails.copy_strategy: Last call: WELCOME10 expires. 'This is your last chance to try with the code + guarantee.' Permission not to buy present.
Gentle close. Door stays open. Migration anchors (L2/L3 story links for GF non-migrators). Lena farewell.

l2_emails.copy_strategy: Welcome + code. Mirror: 'You've tried BCBA, OT, social stories... nothing's clicking.' Lena intro — her sons were L2-like (one trained at 8).
l2_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Deep validation from email 1. No quick fixes.

l2_emails.copy_strategy: Mechanism deep dive: 6-Element Formula MANDATORY. Name 3+ failed methods by name. Explain why each failed (sensory gap). 'Your BCBA built the behavior piece. This adds the sensory layer.'
l2_emails.key_emotional_tactic: THE CRITICAL L2 EMAIL. Must contain ALL 6 elements: symptom recognition, mechanism specificity, BCBA acknowledgment, realistic timeline, 60-day guarantee, permission not to buy.

l2_emails.copy_strategy: 60-day guarantee prominent. 'After everything you've tried, skepticism is smart. 60 days. By your judgment.'
l2_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Permission-not-to-buy is STRONG here. She needs to feel she's deciding.

l2_emails.copy_strategy: Reframe: 'You didn't fail. Your BCBA didn't fail. The approach missed the sensory piece.' BCBA-respectful language.
Science deep-dive: Kelly Mahler quote, SPARK 49% stat, interoception studies. Cite Nicholson 2019, Iqbal 2022.
l2_emails.key_emotional_tactic: L2 wants data. Give her real studies.

l2_emails.copy_strategy: Testimonials — L2-specific journeys. Length matters: 10-15 sentences. Real regression cycles.
Deeper mechanism: prompt-dependency loop, how BSL breaks it. 'Your child has been trained to comply. We train them to FEEL.'
Gentle close. Door open. FAQ path. Lena farewell with sons reference. Sign-off: 'With understanding,'
l2_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Lena's sons reference grounds the peer-trust.

l3_emails.copy_strategy: Welcome + code. Mirror: 'You've stopped counting the years. That's not giving up — that's protecting yourself.' Lena intro — her son took until 8.
l3_emails.key_emotional_tactic: SLOW cadence. E1→E2 gap = 2 days. ZERO urgency. Sign-off: 'With care,'

l3_emails.copy_strategy: Gentle mechanism: 'He's never shown a sign of feeling wet. That's not failure — that's neurology.' Reframe with dignity.
60-day guarantee. 'Not all kids respond. 60 days to know. If nothing shifts, full refund.'
'Late is not never' — Maureen Bennie's son at 9.5. Validate years of effort. 'Whatever progress means for your family is enough.'
l3_emails.key_emotional_tactic: Emotional core of L3 journey. Handle with care.

l3_emails.copy_strategy: Smallest signs that matter: 'A pause. A touch. Looking down. Those ARE progress.'
Add alongside specialty diapers. 'This isn't replacing anything. It adds a body-signal layer alongside.'
Deep dignity: 'His autism isn't being treated. His nervous system is being supported.'
l3_emails.key_emotional_tactic: No push. No urgency. Gentle presence.

l3_emails.copy_strategy: Door stays open. Lena farewell with deep warmth. Sign-off: 'With care,'

welcome.gf_summary: All 8 emails use R1-R6 calibration. 'Whether...Or...If...' sentence structure. Migration anchors embedded in E2-E4 (story links for level identification). Symptom language ONLY — no 'autism,' 'ASD,' 'Level.' Never mention 'adult diapers' or 'non-verbal.' For Sub-Group B (Diagnosis-Rejecters), use 'nervous system differences.' For Sub-Group C (SPD/ADHD, no ASD), mechanism-oriented language without diagnostic labels. No per-email table provided in S02 §4.1 — GF email content must be derived from R1-R6 + L1/L2/L3 base tables, with all level labels stripped.
cross_cutting_notes: Sign-off progression: E1-E2 'Talk soon,' → E3-E5 'With understanding,' → E6+ 'With care,'
WELCOME10 code used across all variants in E1
L3 never receives Pre-K urgency language
L2 must always include BCBA-respect language
L1 is the ONLY level where Pre-K urgency is acceptable
E4 cadence differs from S01 §3.1 (see inconsistencies)

cart_abandonment.name: Cart Abandonment

emails.copy_strategy: Mechanism reminder: 'You added the 3+3 bundle. Here's why parents buy it — it's the cheapest cost-per-wear for the body-signal layer.'
emails.key_emotional_tactic: Recognition triggers R2: 3-5 anchors. Lena voice: 'I saw these were on your mind...'

emails.copy_strategy: Social proof + product value. Three-Family Pattern (one testimonial per level path). Dynamic content: upsell 1-pair cart to 3+3.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: 'Most parents who buy 1 pair come back for 3+3 within a week.'

emails.copy_strategy: 60-day guarantee + permission not to buy. 'No rush. If this isn't the day, that's okay.' Risk reversal = trust building.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: 'Whatever you choose — thanks for reading this far.'

cross_cutting_notes: R1-R6 mandatory
Speed > segmentation
Dynamic content: cart contents drive upsell strategy
NO additional discount E1-E2. E3: optional 10% off (test without first)
Suppression rule: Cart Abandonment SUPPRESSES Welcome Flow E3+ during active cart (per S01 §3.1 Flow 07)

checkout_abandonment.name: Checkout Abandonment

emails.copy_strategy: Friendly reminder + answer last doubts. 'If today isn't right, that's a real answer.' Show exact cart contents with pricing.
Final nudge + 60-day guarantee. Decision-respect tone. 'You decide.' Free shipping reminder.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: 'Try it for 60 days. By your judgment.'

cross_cutting_notes: NO additional discount (highest-intent abandoners)
Cross-level only — no segmentation

browse_abandonment.name: Browse Abandonment

emails.copy_strategy: Mechanism education hook. 'You were looking at something that helps kids who can't feel when they need to go.'
Deep mechanism + product education. 'Here's why the 3-layer system works differently.' One testimonial (Three-Family).
60-day guarantee + gentle close. Door stays open.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: '60 days for your child's nervous system to start connecting.'

cross_cutting_notes: E1-E2: NO direct offer
E3: First offer presentation
Trigger: viewed product pages, no cart created

order_confirmation.name: Order Confirmation

emails.copy_strategy: Tone 50/50 hope/realism (neutral transactional). Order summary, shipping timeline, 60-day guarantee reminder (post-purchase confidence). Lena sign-off: 'You've got this. We've got your back. — Lena.'

cross_cutting_notes: Google 2024 rule: Must be predominantly transactional. NO promotional content (per S01 §3.1 Flow 09)

shipping_out_for_delivery.name: Shipping & Out for Delivery

emails.copy_strategy: Tracking info, delivery estimate, teaser for what happens on arrival. Lena: 'Can't wait for you to try them. — Lena.'
emails.key_emotional_tactic: Excitement building, prep tips for receiving.

emails.copy_strategy: 'Today's the day!' Prep guidance: wash first, no fabric softener, let child unbox. Manage expectations: 'First few wears are about introducing the sensation, not results.'

cross_cutting_notes: S01 §3.1 splits this into two separate flows (10 Shipping Notification + 11 Out for Delivery); S02 §4.6 merges them into one flow with 2 emails.

arrived_welcome.name: Arrived & Welcome

emails.copy_strategy: 'They're here!' First-wear protocol. Education: 'The wetness signal works by letting your child FEEL when they're wet.' What-to-expect timeline: Week 1 awareness, Week 2-4 recognition, Week 4-8 active response. Link to PP-Education resources.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: Gentle upsell if 1-pair buyer (1→3+3, 3+3→5+5 per S01 §3.1).

cross_cutting_notes: S01 §3.1 Flow 12 calls this 'massive upsell opportunity' — flagged as upsell window
Trigger: Delivery confirmed or estimated delivery date

pp_direct_upsell.name: PP-Direct Upsell

emails.copy_strategy: 'Most parents who buy 1 pair come back for 3+3 within a week. Here's why.'
emails.key_emotional_tactic: Bundle comparison table. Math reframe OK.

emails.copy_strategy: 'Your 1 pair is on its way. Here's how the 3+3 bundle complements it.'

cross_cutting_notes: Target: 1-pair buyers only
Tone: Helper, not seller. NEVER 'You didn't buy enough.'
Frame as rotation need
S01 §3.1 Flow 13 says timing is D0/D5; S02 §4.8 says D1/D3 — INCONSISTENCY

pp_level_detection.name: PP-Level Detection

emails.copy_strategy: Framing: 'I've noticed parents in different situations have different experiences. You might recognize yourself in one of these stories...' 3-4 story links targeting L1/L2/L3 scenarios.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: No offer (pure detection). Expected click migration: 40-50% (highest-converting migration touchpoint per S01 §5.2).

cross_cutting_notes: Critical migration flow
Takes priority over ALL other Day 14 content (Welcome E6, PP-Education)
NEVER assign below 70% confidence — stay GF if uncertain
Migration anchors must be organic story recommendations, NOT 'Click here if you're Level X'
4 re-selection buttons with same symptom-based language as pop-up

pp_mary_s_story.name: PP-Mary S. Story

emails.copy_strategy: Founder story — Mary S. (founder) appears ONCE. Cross-level calibration (R1-R6). 'I started BrightKidCo because my own child couldn't feel the signal...' Signed as Mary S., not Lena.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: No offer in this email — pure brand building. Preceding email (PP-Upsell E2) introduces Mary.

cross_cutting_notes: Mary S. appears ONLY in this dedicated mail (NOT in Lena's regular mails)
S01 §3.1 places this in Phase 1 (Flow 15); S02 §4.10 listing order implies Phase 2 — INCONSISTENCY (also flagged in S01 Appendix C8)

pp_education_d0_21.name: PP-Education D0-21

emails.copy_strategy: Theme: 'Your first wear.' Put on like regular underwear. No expectations. Just observe.
Theme: 'What to look for.' Micro-signs: pause, touch, look down, shorter dry periods.
Theme: 'Week two reality.' Progress isn't linear. Regression is normal. Don't stop.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: 'Some kids react on day 3. Others day 30. Both normal.'

emails.copy_strategy: Theme: 'Celebrate the small wins.' 'He looked at the wet spot!' — share wins, encourage logging.

cross_cutting_notes: Cross-level R1-R6
Level-aware content blocks (if tag known)
Coaching + encouragement tone
1-2 parent quotes per email
NO outcome promises

pp_extended_upsell.name: PP-Extended Upsell

emails.copy_strategy: Bundle upgrade. Level-differentiated: L1: 'Complete kit' urgency | L2: 'BCBA complement' | L3: 'When you're ready'
emails.primary_lever: Varies by level (L1: Pre-K urgency; L2: BCBA complement; L3: very low CTA)

emails.copy_strategy: Accessory cross-sell. Cross-level R1-R6 (Wash Bag, Magic Targets).
Final upsell. Guarantee reinforcement, door open.

cross_cutting_notes: L3: NEVER push 5+5. 1 Pair → 3+3 at most, organic only
S02 §4.12 labels this 'Level-Aware' (not 'Level-Specific') because body is cross-level with per-level framing

pp_mid_check_in.name: PP-Mid Check-In

emails.copy_strategy: Three-Family Testimonial (Sarah/Lisa/Maria stories) + reply-to-Lena invitation. Celebrate any progress, normalize absence of progress.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: 3 different moms share their child's story at 30 days. Common thread: each is somewhere different on the curve. Permission not to buy more pairs yet.

emails.copy_strategy: Theme: 'How's it going?' Reply-to-this-email for support. Celebrate any progress.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: High-trust invitation. No offers in E1.

emails.copy_strategy: Theme: 'At the 60-day mark.' Guarantee reminder (earliest use window).
Theme: 'Looking ahead.' Replenishment timing preview. 'You might need more pairs soon.'

cross_cutting_notes: Supportive peer tone. 'Reply to this email' — high-trust invitation
S01 §3.2 Flow 18 timing is 'D30-60'; S02 §4.13 says D45/D60/D75 — INCONSISTENCY

pp_extended_education.name: PP-Extended Education
pp_extended_education.structure_note: S02 §4.14 lists themes only — no per-email table or day timings. 5 themes per level × 4 levels = 20 emails.
cross_cutting_notes: S01 §3.3 places this in Phase 3; S01 Appendix C6 flags 'PP-Extended Edu: Phase 3 vs Phase 2 (Demands vs Strategy)'
Trigger: D22+ with level tag + have bought (per S01 §3.3)
Per-email timings not specified in S02 §4.14

pp_support_at_risk.name: PP-Support At-Risk

emails.copy_strategy: 'I noticed you haven't opened recent emails. Just checking in.'
'Here's how to get the most out of your pairs.'
'Your child's nervous system is learning even when it doesn't show.'
'Your 60-day guarantee is still active.'

cross_cutting_notes: Trigger: 3-7 days no opens post-purchase
NEVER 'We miss you' or guilt-tripping
If level tag known: adjust tone (L3: extra gentle)
No upsells

replenishment.name: Replenishment A/B/C

emails.copy_strategy: Wash cycles reminder.
Consistency matters.
Guarantee threshold.
'Time to rotate.'
Bundle upgrade to 5+5.
'Long haul support.'
Accessory refresh.

cross_cutting_notes: Tone: Practical reminder, not sales. 'Training underwear works best when you rotate pairs.'
L3 rule: NEVER push 5+5 (per pp_extended_upsell cross-cutting)
S01 §3.2 timing is generic 'post-purchase D30-60'; S02 gives specific day sequences D14/D30/D60, D75/D90, D150/D180 — INCONSISTENCY in granularity

review_request.name: Review Request

emails.copy_strategy: 'Has BrightKidCo made a difference? Share your experience.' Direct review link + 1-2 leading questions.
Follow-up if no review. 'Your experience helps other parents decide.'

cross_cutting_notes: 'Other parents need to hear what worked (and didn't) for your child.'
S01 §3.2 says 'Post-purchase D21-30'; S02 §4.17 says D30/D60 — INCONSISTENCY

winback_path_a.name: Winback Path A
winback_path_a.structure_note: S02 §4.18 lists themes per level only — no per-email tables or day timings. 3 themes per level × 4 levels = 12 emails. Trigger: 90 days no engagement (post-purchaser).
cross_cutting_notes: Trigger: 90 days no engagement (post-purchaser)
Highest L3 conversion cell
Mechanism reminder critical for L2
S01 §3.2 says Phase 2; S01 Appendix C7 flags 'Winback Path A: Phase 2 vs Phase 3'
Suppression priority: Winback (Path A or B) pauses ALL nurture flows while active; overrides Welcome, PP-Education, Replenishment

winback_path_b.name: Winback Path B
winback_path_b.structure_note: S02 §4.19 says: same structure as Path A. 3 themes per level × 4 levels = 12 emails. Trigger: 60 days no purchase (never-bought).
cross_cutting_notes: Trigger: 60 days no purchase (never-bought)
Lower conversion than Path A
Non-buyers need stronger mechanism proof
FAQ path after winback completion

faq_objection_library.name: FAQ/Objection Library
faq_objection_library.structure_note: S02 §4.20: 'Level-Specific Blocks' — cross-level body with per-level content blocks. S01 §3.3 calls this 'Cross-Level' and notes 'Current strategy docs say 4 variants — this is wrong per Demands' (Appendix C4). Each email uses 4-step reframe (Name → Validate → Reframe → Absolve).

emails.copy_strategy: Primary objection: 'Tried Everything' (D1). 4-step reframe.
Primary objection: 'Too Old/Severe' (D4). 4-step reframe.
Primary objection: 'GI/Withholding' (B4). 4-step reframe.
Primary objection: 'Worth $30-50?' (C3). 4-step reframe.
emails.key_emotional_tactic: Defensive → Valued. NOTE: L3 NEVER math reframe — math reframe applies to L1-L2 only.

emails.copy_strategy: Primary objection: 'BCBA Compatibility' (B2). 4-step reframe.
Primary objection: 'Hope-Crash Fear' (D3). 4-step reframe.

cross_cutting_notes: Phase 3 trigger: Welcome end + no purchase
Each email: 1 objection → 4-step reframe (Name → Validate → Reframe → Absolve)
Per-level content blocks with cross-level body

DISCREPANCY: S01 §3.3 says 'must be cross-level, NOT 4 variants' (per Demands); S02 §4.20 labels 'Level-Specific Blocks' — labels differ but substance (cross-level body + level-specific blocks) is reconcilable. S01 Appendix C4 flags as P0 contradiction.

referral_flow.name: Referral Flow

emails.copy_strategy: 'Know another parent who's struggling? Share BrightKidCo.' Incentive: discount on next purchase.
Referral ask reinforcement. Lena voice: 'When something works for our kids, we tell each other.'
Final reminder + dual-sided incentive.

cross_cutting_notes: Phase 3 trigger
Post-purchase + satisfaction indicator
S02 §4.21 narrative mentions 3 emails but no formal per-email table — timings inferred from S01 §3.3 trigger description

sunset.name: Sunset

emails.copy_strategy: Re-permission check. 'Do you still want to hear from us?' One-click preference update.
Final farewell. No response = suppressed for 6-12 months.

cross_cutting_notes: Phase 3. 120d inactive (post-Winback completion)
After sunset: Tag `unengaged = True`, add to Master Suppression List (per S01 §3.3 Flow 27)
HIGHEST PRIORITY — suppresses ALL other flows unconditionally
Trigger per S01 §3.3 Flow 27: 120 days no open AND no click AND no purchase AND received 20+ emails
S02 §4.22 trigger is '120d inactive (post-Winback completion)' — INCONSISTENCY with S01

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3 enumerates 27 flows (Flows 01-27). S02 §4 enumerates 22 flows (4.1-4.22). Reconcilable mapping: S01's 'Site Abandonment' (Flow 05) has no S02 equivalent; S01's 'Shipping Notification' (Flow 10) + 'Out for Delivery' (Flow 11) are merged into S02 4.6 'Shipping & Out for Delivery.' All other flows map 1:1 but with renumbering.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: Welcome Flow cadence: S02 slower than S01
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.1 table gives cadence D0/D1/D3/D5/D7/D10/D14/D21 for all 4 variants. S02 §4.1 (L1/L2/L3 tables) gives D0/D1/D3/D7/D10/D14/D17/D21 — i.e., E4 through E7 are 2-3 days LATER in S02. S02 §4.1 L3 has an additional quirk: E1→E2 gap is 2 days (D0→D2) and E3 is D5, then E4 catches up to D7. S01 has no per-level cadence distinction.
S01 §3.1 Flow 06 says '2h / 24h / 72h.' S02 §4.4 says '24 hours / 3 days / 7 days.' These are completely different cadence schemes.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: PP-Direct Upsell timing: D0/D5 vs D1/D3
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.1 Flow 13 says 'D0 and D5 after purchase.' S02 §4.8 says Day 1 and Day 3.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: PP-Mid Check-In timing: D30-60 vs D45/D60/D75
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.2 Flow 18 says 'Post-purchase D30-60.' S02 §4.13 gives explicit D45/D60/D75.
S01 §3.2 Flow 19 gives vague 'Based on purchase count/recency' (A=3 emails, B=2, C=2). S02 §4.16 gives explicit per-email day sequences: A=D14/D30/D60, B=D75/D90, C=D150/D180.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: Review Request timing: D21-30 vs D30/D60
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.2 Flow 20 says 'Post-purchase D21-30.' S02 §4.17 says D30 for E1 and D60 for E2.
S02 §4.22 says '120d inactive (post-Winback completion).' S01 §3.3 Flow 27 specifies: '120 days no open AND no click AND no purchase AND received 20+ emails.' Different trigger logic.
S01 §3.1 places Flow 15 in Phase 1 (Months 1-2). S02 §4.10 lists it between PP-Level Detection (4.9) and PP-Education D0-21 (4.11), implying Phase 2 placement. Known conflict per S01 Appendix C8.

S01 §3.3 says 'must be cross-level, NOT 4 variants' and flags in Appendix C4 (P0) that 'Current strategy docs say 4 variants — this is wrong per Demands.' S02 §4.20 labels it 'Level-Specific Blocks' with 6 emails, cross-level body, per-level content blocks. Labels differ but substance is reconcilable if 'Level-Specific Blocks' means cross-level body with per-level content snippets (matches Demands intent).

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: Welcome Flow GF variant: no per-email table in S02
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S02 §4.1 provides detailed per-email tables for L1, L2, L3 (8 emails each = 24 rows). GF variant has only a summary paragraph covering all 8 emails collectively. To produce 8 GF email rows, content must be derived from R1-R6 + L1/L2/L3 base with level labels stripped.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: PP-Extended Education (4.14), Winback A (4.18), Winback B (4.19): themes only
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: These three level-specific flows in S02 §4 list themes per level (no per-email tables, no day timings). To produce 5+3+3 = 11 email rows per level (44 total across 4 levels), timing must be inferred. S01 §3.3 indicates D22+ trigger for PP-Extended Education.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: PP-Extended Education phase: Phase 2 vs Phase 3
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.3 puts Flow 22 in Phase 3 (Months 5-6). S01 Appendix C6 flags this as known conflict ('PP-Extended Edu: Phase 3 vs Phase 2 (Demands vs Strategy)'). S02 §4.14 doesn't state phase explicitly.

inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.topic: Winback Path A phase: Phase 2 vs Phase 3
inconsistencies_s02_vs_s01.detail: S01 §3.2 says Phase 2 per Demands ('Phase 2 per Demands — not Phase 3. Discrepancy noted'). S01 Appendix C7: 'Winback Path A: Phase 2 vs Phase 3.' S02 §4.18 doesn't state phase explicitly.

S01 §3.1 Flow 15 says 'Founder story — Mary S.'s personal journey with her own autistic child.' S02 §4.10 attributes same story but adds 'I started BrightKidCo because my own child couldn't feel the signal...' as the verbatim opening line. Phrasing consistent in spirit but S02 supplies actual copy.

S01 §1 says '~110+ unique emails deployed.' S02 §4 sums to: Welcome 32 + Cart 3 + Checkout 2 + Browse 3 + Order 1 + Shipping/OFD 2 + Arrived 1 + PP-Direct 2 + PP-Level Detect 1 + Mary S. 1 + PP-Edu D0-21 4 + PP-Ext Upsell 3 + PP-Mid Check-In 3 + PP-Ext Edu 20 + PP-Support 4 + Replenishment 7 + Review 2 + Winback A 12 + Winback B 12 + FAQ 6 + Referral 3 + Sunset 2 = 130 emails. S01 '~110+' is a rough lower bound; S02 is more precise.

phase_assignment_known.pp_mary_s_story_phase_disputed: S01 §3.1 says Phase 1; S02 §4.10 ordering implies Phase 2 — INCONSISTENCY I09
